name: KrillApp.Trigger.Color
title: Color Trigger
description: Triggers an alarm and executes child nodes when the parent DataPoint's color value falls within a configured RGB range. Decomposes the color integer into R, G, B channels and checks each against min/max bounds. Fires when all three channels are within their respective ranges. Sets node state to WARN on match.
shortDescription: Triggers when a DataPoint color falls within a configured RGB range.
llmPurpose: Evaluates whether a DataPoint color value falls within per-channel RGB min/max ranges and triggers child execution and alarm state when matched.
llmBehavior: reads upstream DataPoint snapshot color value
compares each channel against min/max range
sets node state to WARN on match
llmOutputs: executes child nodes when color is within range
llmCreationHints: Create a Color trigger when the user wants to detect a specific color range from a COLOR DataPoint. Configure per-channel RGB min/max bounds. Useful for pH indicator monitoring, water quality sensors, and colorimetric test analysis.

llmConnectionHints.role: conditional trigger that fires when DataPoint color value falls within configured RGB range

llmExamples: Alert me when the pH indicator turns red (R > 180, G < 80, B < 60).
Trigger a webhook when the water color shifts to green.
Warn me when the test strip color matches the target range.
